extractions.problem: Flux balance analysis (FBA) assumes evolutionary optimality to predict metabolic fluxes, but this assumption may not hold for genetically engineered knockouts not exposed to long-term evolutionary pressure.
extractions.method: Introduced minimization of metabolic adjustment (MOMA) using quadratic programming to predict knockout flux distributions closest to wild-type, compared with FBA using linear programming.
extractions.finding: MOMA showed significantly higher correlation with experimental flux data for E. coli pyruvate kinase mutant than FBA, and was further supported by knockout growth rate data.
extractions.dataset: E. coli JM101 (wild-type), E. coli PB25 (pyruvate kinase mutant)
extractions.metric: Correlation with experimental flux data
extractions.result: MOMA significantly higher correlation than FBA for knockout mutant

extractions.problem: How protein complexes around transmembrane proteins control the polarized architecture of epithelial cells and cellular junctions in a whole animal model.
extractions.method: Review of genetic and molecular analysis of epithelial polarity and cellular junctions in Drosophila.
extractions.finding: Membrane asymmetry and junction differentiation are controlled by complexes assembling around DE-cadherin, Crumbs, and Neurexin IV, but mechanisms of assembly and polarized distribution remain unclear.
extractions.dataset: Drosophila melanogaster epithelial cells

extractions.problem: Rebuilding whole genome 3D structures from sparse haploid single-cell Hi-C contact data.
extractions.method: Minimal Go-model approach for genome structure reconstruction from single-cell Hi-C data.
extractions.dataset: Haploid single-cell Hi-C data

extractions.problem: Comprehensive genomic analysis of single cells is needed to assess clonal evolution in heterogeneous populations such as micrometastatic cells.
extractions.method: PCR-based global DNA amplification from single cells followed by comparative genomic hybridization (CGH), loss of heterozygosity (LOH), and DNA sequencing.
extractions.finding: The technique reliably detects gross and fine-scale copy number variations (monosomy X, trisomy 21, tumor-specific alterations) from single cells, with congruent CGH, LOH, and sequencing data attesting to fidelity.
extractions.dataset: Single human cells (male, Down's syndrome, tumor cells, bone marrow micrometastatic cells from cancer patients)
extractions.result: Reliable detection of monosomic X, trisomic 21, and tumor-specific deletions/amplifications from single cells

extractions.problem: The role of host red blood cell deformability in determining circulatory characteristics and transmission competence of Plasmodium falciparum gametocytes is poorly understood.
extractions.method: Transgenic parasite line for 3D live imaging, in vitro capillary assays, and 3D finite element whole-cell modeling of gametocyte-infected red blood cells.
extractions.finding: Immature gametocytes show decreased RBC deformability (enabling sequestration) followed by rapid restoration in mature stages, and morphological changes also play a role in tissue distribution.
extractions.dataset: Plasmodium falciparum gametocyte-infected red blood cells
extractions.metric: Cell deformability, capillary transit
extractions.baseline: Uninfected red blood cells, asexual-stage infected RBCs
extractions.result: Deformability decrease in immature then restoration in mature gametocytes

extractions.problem: How the KCNQ1-G589D mutation, which disrupts AKAP-mediated PKA/PP1 targeting to the IKs channel, leads to ventricular arrhythmia during sympathetic stimulation.
extractions.method: Integrative computational models of beta-adrenergic signaling, myocyte excitation-contraction coupling, and 3D action potential propagation in a rabbit ventricular wedge.
extractions.finding: The mutation alone does not prolong QT, but coupled with beta-adrenergic stimulation it induces QT prolongation and afterdepolarizations; increasing heart rate is protective.
extractions.dataset: Rabbit ventricular wedge model (computational)
extractions.metric: QT interval, transmural dispersion of repolarization
extractions.result: QT prolongation and afterdepolarizations under beta-adrenergic stimulation with mutation

extractions.problem: CRISPR-Cas9 genome editing generates DNA double-strand breaks whose full spectrum of chromosomal structural consequences is poorly characterized.
extractions.method: Single-cell whole-genome sequencing of model cells and clinically relevant cells after CRISPR-Cas9 editing, combined with microscopy for nuclear defects.
extractions.finding: CRISPR-Cas9 editing generates micronuclei and chromosome bridges that initiate chromothripsis, extensive chromosome rearrangement, as an on-target consequence.
extractions.dataset: Model cell lines, clinically relevant human cells
extractions.result: Chromothripsis detected as on-target consequence of CRISPR-Cas9 DSBs

extractions.problem: Whether the spatial periodicity of entorhinal grid cell firing patterns corresponds to intrinsic electrophysiological properties of entorhinal neurons along the dorsal-ventral axis.
extractions.method: In vitro whole-cell patch-clamp recordings from rat entorhinal cortex layer II neurons at different dorsal-ventral positions.
extractions.finding: Subthreshold membrane potential oscillation frequency scales with anatomical position along the dorsal-ventral axis, matching grid cell field spacing and supporting a model of grid cell generation.
extractions.dataset: Rat entorhinal cortex layer II neurons
extractions.metric: Subthreshold oscillation frequency
extractions.result: Oscillation frequency correlates with dorsal-ventral position and grid field spacing

extractions.problem: Glucokinase plays a key role in glucose homeostasis; activating it pharmacologically could treat type 2 diabetes.
extractions.method: Discovery and characterization of small-molecule glucokinase activators (GKAs) that increase glucose affinity and Vmax, tested in isolated rodent islets and multiple rodent diabetes models.
extractions.finding: GKAs augmented hepatic glucose metabolism and insulin secretion, lowered blood glucose, improved glucose tolerance, and increased hepatic glucose uptake in rodent type 2 diabetes models.
extractions.dataset: Rodent pancreatic islets, multiple rodent models of type 2 diabetes
extractions.metric: Blood glucose levels, glucose tolerance test results, hepatic glucose uptake
extractions.result: Lowered blood glucose and improved glucose tolerance in multiple rodent diabetes models

extractions.problem: Evaluating whether fish embryo acute toxicity (FET) testing and RTgill-W1 cell line assays can serve as in vitro alternatives for whole-effluent toxicity (WET) testing.
extractions.method: Compared LC50 from fathead minnow FET test and EC50 from RTgill-W1 cells against fish larvae acute toxicity for 12 WET-relevant chemicals, including cardiotoxicity and hypoosmotic condition evaluations.
extractions.finding: Significant correlation between FET and larvae LC50 but not RTgill-W1 EC50 and FET LC50; hypoosmotic conditions improved RTgill-W1 sensitivity; both in vitro models showed good predictability overall.
extractions.dataset: Fathead minnow embryos, RTgill-W1 cells, 12 WET-relevant chemicals (Cd, Cu, Ni, ammonia, 3,4-DCA, benzalkonium chloride, etc.)
extractions.metric: LC50, EC50, correlation between in vitro and in vivo
extractions.baseline: Fish larvae acute toxicity test (in vivo)
extractions.result: Significant correlation between FET and larvae LC50; both in vitro models show good predictability

extractions.problem: Current AFM techniques for measuring cell mechanical properties require substrate adhesion, making them unsuitable for nonadherent cells like leukocytes.
extractions.method: Developed AFM method using microfabricated wells to immobilize nonadherent cells, applied Hertzian elastic model to compare deformability of myeloid and lymphoid leukemia cells and neutrophils.
extractions.finding: Myeloid HL60 cells are 18x stiffer than lymphoid Jurkat cells and 6x stiffer than neutrophils, which may contribute to leukostasis in acute leukemia.
extractions.dataset: Human HL60 (myeloid leukemia), Jurkat (lymphoid leukemia), neutrophils
extractions.metric: Young's modulus (E_infinity)
extractions.result: HL60: 855±670 Pa, Jurkat: 48±35 Pa, neutrophils: 156±87 Pa

extractions.problem: Existing equations for predicting tissue-to-plasma partition coefficients (Kpu) are inaccurate for moderate-to-strong bases due to unique electrostatic interactions with tissue acidic phospholipids.
extractions.method: Developed a mechanistic equation incorporating drug-acidic phospholipid electrostatic interactions estimated from blood cell binding data, validated for 28 moderate-to-strong bases across 11 rat tissues.
extractions.finding: The new equation achieved 89% of predictions within 3-fold of experimental values across 11 tissues, compared to 45% for published equations; brain and lung predictions were less accurate.
extractions.dataset: 28 moderate-to-strong bases (pKa >= 7), rat tissues (adipose, bone, gut, heart, kidney, liver, muscle, pancreas, skin, spleen, thymus, brain, lung)
extractions.metric: Percentage of Kpu predictions within 3-fold of experimental values
extractions.result: 89% within 3-fold vs 45% for published equations

extractions.problem: Whole-blood gene expression studies of sepsis have not resolved transcriptional changes at the level of specific immune cell types.
extractions.method: Single-cell RNA-sequencing of PBMCs (106,545 cells) and dendritic cells (19,806 cells) from sepsis patients (n=29) and controls (n=36) across three cohorts, validated on 18 public cohorts (n=1,467).
extractions.finding: Identified a unique expanded CD14+ monocyte state in sepsis that distinguishes patients from controls across multiple independent cohorts and disease etiologies.
extractions.dataset: Sepsis patients (n=29), controls (n=36), validation: 18 cohorts (n=1,467)
extractions.metric: Discriminatory power for sepsis vs control classification
extractions.result: CD14+ monocyte state validated across 18 cohorts, n=1,467 subjects

extractions.problem: Understanding electrocatalysts and catalyst layers for proton exchange membrane (PEM) fuel cells.
extractions.dataset: PEM fuel cell catalyst systems

extractions.problem: Whether the relative contributions of phylogenetic groups to dissolved organic matter (DOM) utilization depend solely on their relative abundance in bacterial communities.
extractions.method: Combined microautoradiography with fluorescent rRNA-targeted oligonucleotide probe hybridization (MICRO-FISH) to track DOM utilization by different phylogenetic groups.
extractions.finding: DOM utilization differed across phylogenetic groups and did not correlate with relative abundance; no single group dominated all DOM consumption, suggesting diverse assemblages are essential for complete DOM degradation.
extractions.dataset: Natural marine bacterial assemblages from estuarine and coastal environments
extractions.metric: Relative representation in DOM-consuming assemblage vs whole community
extractions.baseline: Expected utilization based on relative abundance (null hypothesis)
extractions.result: Cytophaga-Flavobacter overrepresented in chitin/protein consumption; alpha-Proteobacteria dominated amino acid consumption

extractions.problem: Whether dendritic filtering causes distant synapses to have less efficacy than proximal ones at the soma of hippocampal pyramidal neurons.
extractions.method: Dual whole-cell patch-clamp recordings from soma and apical dendrite of CA1 pyramidal neurons combined with multi-compartmental computer modeling.
extractions.finding: Dendritic EPSP amplitude increases with distance from soma, counterbalancing dendritic filtering so that somatic EPSP amplitude is largely independent of synapse location, primarily due to progressive increase in synaptic conductance.
extractions.dataset: Rat hippocampal CA1 pyramidal neurons
extractions.metric: Somatic EPSP amplitude, dendritic EPSP amplitude vs distance
extractions.result: Somatic EPSP amplitude independent of synapse location

extractions.problem: The molecular identity and functional properties of light-activated channels in Drosophila photoreceptors, particularly the role of the trp gene whose mutation causes response decline during prolonged light.
extractions.method: Whole-cell patch-clamp recordings from Drosophila photoreceptors comparing wild-type and trp mutant responses, with pharmacological Ca2+ channel blockade (La3+).
extractions.finding: Wild-type photoreceptors have at least two classes of light-sensitive channels; trp mutation and La3+ selectively abolish a class with high Ca2+ permeability required for sustained excitation.
extractions.dataset: Drosophila melanogaster photoreceptors (wild-type and trp mutant)
extractions.result: trp mutation selectively abolishes high-Ca2+-permeability channel class

extractions.problem: How kin selection theory explains the evolution of cooperation and altruism in social insect colonies, including the transition to superorganismal organization.
extractions.method: Review and theoretical analysis of kin selection theory applied to social insect evolution.
extractions.finding: Social insect colonies offer an accessible model for understanding major evolutionary transitions to higher-level organisms, spanning the full range from solitary to superorganismal.
extractions.dataset: Social insect colonies (Hymenoptera, Isoptera)

extractions.problem: Genomics alone is insufficient to identify therapeutic options for the majority of patients with advanced cancer, necessitating functional drug screening approaches.
extractions.method: Integrated whole-exome sequencing with high-throughput drug screening on patient-derived tumor organoids and PDX models from 769 enrolled patients (56 organoid cultures, 19 PDX models).
extractions.finding: High-throughput drug screening on patient-derived organoids identified effective drugs and combinations validated in 3D cultures and PDX models, providing personalized therapeutic options.
extractions.dataset: 769 cancer patients (56 organoid cultures, 19 PDX models; uterine and colon cancers highlighted)
extractions.metric: Drug sensitivity, validation in 3D cultures and PDX
extractions.result: Effective drugs identified for 4 cases where genomics alone was insufficient

extractions.problem: Modeling hippocampal neural circuitry using electrophysiological data from patch-clamp and dual intracellular recordings.
extractions.method: Whole-cell patch clamp and dual intracellular recordings in vitro for hippocampal circuit modeling.
extractions.dataset: Hippocampal neurons (in vitro)

extractions.problem: Understanding the mechanisms of cell differentiation using plant trichomes as a model system.
extractions.dataset: Plant trichomes

extractions.problem: Existing blood-brain barrier (BBB) models lack the ability to recapitulate complex BBB functions and model inheritable neurological disorders using patient-specific cells.
extractions.method: Created human BBB-Chip using iPSC-derived brain microvascular endothelial-like cells, astrocytes, and neurons on Organ-Chip microfluidic technology.
extractions.finding: BBB-Chip exhibited physiologically relevant TEER, accurately predicted blood-to-brain permeability, protected neural cells from plasma toxicity upon whole blood perfusion, and patient-derived iPSCs predicted disease-specific barrier disruption.
extractions.dataset: Human iPSC-derived cells, patient-derived iPSCs from individuals with neurological diseases
extractions.metric: Transendothelial electrical resistance (TEER), blood-to-brain permeability prediction
extractions.result: Accurate permeability prediction; disease-specific transporter deficiency and barrier disruption detected

extractions.problem: Understanding the structural organization of pulmonary surfactant membranes and films, and how lipid-protein interactions determine surfactant function.
extractions.method: Review of in vivo and in vitro studies on surfactant structure, membrane polymorphism, and lipid-protein interactions, with emphasis on surfactant protein roles.
extractions.finding: Surfactant structure involves dynamic membrane polymorphism controlled by specific lipid-lipid and lipid-protein interactions; in vitro reconstituted systems have limited structural complexity compared to native surfactant.
extractions.dataset: Pulmonary surfactant (alveolar pneumocytes, lamellar bodies)

extractions.problem: Developing a kinetic model for whole-cell biocatalytic production of D-p-hydroxyphenylglycine using separately and co-expressed D-hydantoinase and N-carbamoylase.
extractions.method: Kinetic modeling incorporating substrate dissolution, enzymatic conversion, racemization, and membrane transport; parameters evaluated at different pH; model simulation compared with experimental data.
extractions.finding: The kinetic model accurately describes both separate and co-expressed enzyme systems; racemization and intermediate transport limit the separate system at neutral pH, while co-expression eliminates intermediate accumulation.
extractions.dataset: Bacillus stearothermophilus SD1 (D-hydantoinase), Agrobacterium tumefaciens NRRL B11291 (N-carbamoylase) whole cells
extractions.metric: Model fit to experimental reaction data
extractions.result: Co-expressed system showed improved performance with negligible intermediate accumulation

extractions.problem: The cellular origins of newly regenerated cardiac muscle after injury in zebrafish are unknown due to lack of lineage-tracing technology.
extractions.method: Genetic fate-mapping using Cre-recombinase-based lineage tracing of gata4+ and cmlc2+ cardiomyocytes, combined with optical voltage mapping in whole ventricles.
extractions.finding: Existing cardiomyocytes, particularly gata4+ cells, are the primary source of regenerated cardiac muscle; electrical conduction is re-established between existing and regenerated cardiomyocytes within 2-4 weeks.
extractions.dataset: Zebrafish (ventricular apex resection model)
extractions.metric: Labeled cardiac muscle in regenerate, electrical conduction restoration timing
extractions.result: gata4+ cardiomyocytes contribute to most regenerated cardiac muscle; conduction restored 2-4 weeks post-injury

extractions.problem: Whether neurons are lost in diabetic mouse retinas and whether such loss involves apoptosis, given limited knowledge in mouse models of diabetic retinopathy.
extractions.method: Streptozotocin-induced diabetes in C57Bl/6 mice studied over 14 weeks with morphometric analysis, TUNEL, activated caspase-3 detection, and electron microscopy.
extractions.finding: Diabetic mice showed ~20-25% fewer cells in the ganglion cell layer at 14 weeks, with evidence of apoptotic cell death (TUNEL+, caspase-3+, chromatin margination).
extractions.dataset: C57Bl/6 mice, streptozotocin-induced diabetes model
extractions.metric: Ganglion cell layer cell count, retinal layer thickness
extractions.result: ~20-25% fewer ganglion cell layer cells at 14 weeks; significant decrease in retinal thickness at 10 weeks

extractions.problem: Generating human induced pluripotent stem cells without genetic modification by direct delivery of reprogramming proteins.
extractions.method: Direct delivery of reprogramming proteins to human cells.
extractions.dataset: Human somatic cells

extractions.problem: Existing numerical simulators for single-porosity reservoirs cannot model multiphase flow in naturally fractured (double-porosity) reservoirs.
extractions.method: Developed a 3D, multiple-well numerical simulator for two-phase water-oil flow in fractured reservoirs extending Warren-Root single-phase equations, solved by finite difference method.
extractions.finding: Imbibition is significant for oil recovery from matrix blocks in fractured reservoirs; the simulator handles uniform and non-uniform fracture distributions and water-oil displacement.
extractions.dataset: Conceptual models: five-spot reservoir quadrant, five-well dipping reservoir with water drive
extractions.metric: Water-oil displacement, oil recovery
extractions.result: Demonstrated significance of imbibition for oil recovery in fractured reservoirs

extractions.problem: Optimizing integrated solid oxide fuel cell (SOFC) systems requires improved thermomechanical management accounting for interactions among all system components.
extractions.method: 3D coupled thermofluid-thermomechanical transient computational model of complete SOFC system including stack, pre-reformer, air pre-heater, and afterburner, validated against measurements.
extractions.finding: The computational model enables investigation of heating-up concepts, efficiency gains, and identification of thermomechanically critical regions, replacing prohibitive experiments.
extractions.dataset: Forschungszentrum Jülich integrated SOFC module
extractions.metric: Thermomechanical stress, temperature distribution
extractions.result: Model predictions validated against experimental measurements

extractions.problem: How membrane properties and intracellular resistivity of the dendritic tree determine voltage attenuation and synaptic integration in neocortical pyramidal neurons.
extractions.method: Simultaneous whole-cell patch-clamp recordings from soma and apical dendrite of layer 5 pyramidal neurons combined with morphologically realistic compartmental models.
extractions.finding: Intracellular resistivity is relatively low (~70-100 Ohm-cm) but nonuniformly distributed Cs-sensitive resting conductances create a 'leaky' apical dendrite that differentially influences integration of spatially segregated synaptic inputs.
extractions.dataset: Neocortical layer 5 pyramidal neurons (rat)
extractions.metric: Voltage attenuation, EPSP integral and peak, intracellular resistivity
extractions.result: Intracellular resistivity ~70-100 Ohm-cm; nonuniform conductances significantly increase voltage attenuation

extractions.problem: Current IAV vaccines in pigs provide homosubtypic protection but fail against heterologous infections; novel prime-boost strategies are needed.
extractions.method: Compared intradermal T-cell epitope DNA vaccine (PigMatrix EDV), intramuscular inactivated whole virus vaccine (FluSure XP), and heterologous prime-boost in 39 pigs challenged with pandemic H1N1 at 9 weeks.
extractions.finding: The heterologous prime-boost (epitope DNA + inactivated vaccine) provided the best protection with reduced lung lesions, no IAV antigen in lung tissue at necropsy, and highest IFN-gamma secreting cells.
extractions.dataset: 39 IAV-free pigs (3-week-old), pandemic H1N1 challenge
extractions.metric: Macroscopic lung lesion scores, IAV antigen in lung tissue, IFN-gamma secreting cells, seroconversion
extractions.baseline: Inactivated vaccine alone (INACT-INACT), unvaccinated challenged controls
extractions.result: EPITOPE-INACT: no IAV antigen in lung, reduced lesion scores; INACT-INACT: bimodal response

extractions.problem: Updating the neurodevelopmental model of schizophrenia in light of new genomic, imaging, and stem cell findings since 2005.
extractions.method: Selective review of longitudinal population studies, genetic variant analyses (common and rare/CNVs), post-mortem gene expression, and developmental brain imaging studies.
extractions.finding: Genetic variants associated with schizophrenia are diagnostically nonspecific (shared with autism, epilepsy); dimensional rather than categorical psychosis concept is supported; placental pathology and iPSC neuronal phenotyping are promising new directions.
extractions.dataset: Whole-population longitudinal studies, post-mortem brain samples, developmental brain imaging cohorts

extractions.problem: Accurate parameter identification of photovoltaic cell and module models is needed for evaluation, control, and maximum power point tracking of PV systems.
extractions.method: Proposed performance-guided JAYA (PGJAYA) algorithm with probability-based self-adaptive evolution strategies and chaotic perturbation mechanism, tested on single diode, double diode, and PV module models.
extractions.finding: PGJAYA consistently obtains the most accurate parameters with strong robustness across all compared algorithms and performs well at different irradiance and temperature conditions.
extractions.dataset: Three standard PV model datasets (single diode, double diode, PV module), experimental data from different PV module types
extractions.metric: Root mean square error (RMSE), robustness (statistical tests)
extractions.result: Most accurate parameters with best robustness among all compared algorithms

extractions.problem: Developing effective sentence embedding representations for information retrieval and document matching tasks.
extractions.method: LSTM-RNN trained on user click-through data from a commercial web search engine for sentence embedding, applied to web document retrieval.
extractions.finding: LSTM-RNN automatically detects salient keywords and allocates them to different cells by topic; the resulting embeddings significantly outperform existing state-of-the-art methods for web document retrieval.
extractions.dataset: Commercial web search engine click-through data
extractions.metric: Document retrieval accuracy (NDCG implied)
extractions.result: Significantly outperforms existing SOTA and Paragraph Vector for web document retrieval

extractions.problem: The contribution of the serotonin system to autism spectrum disorder (ASD) pathophysiology remains incompletely understood despite hyperserotonemia being present in >25% of affected children.
extractions.method: Review of whole blood serotonin studies, neuroimaging, postmortem analyses, genetic linkage/association studies, and SERT Gly56Ala knock-in mouse model characterization.
extractions.finding: Multiple converging lines of evidence implicate the serotonin system in ASD, including heritable hyperserotonemia, rare SERT variants increasing serotonin uptake, and knock-in mouse models recapitulating social/communication/repetitive behavioral deficits.
extractions.dataset: ASD patients/families, founder population, SERT Gly56Ala knock-in mice
extractions.metric: Whole blood serotonin levels, heritability, behavioral assays
extractions.result: Hyperserotonemia in >25% of ASD; SERT Gly56Ala mouse recapitulates biomarker and behavioral phenotypes

extractions.problem: The mechanism by which cells with impaired mitochondrial electron transport chain (ETC) show increased retention of the hypoxia imaging agent Cu(atsm) is not demonstrated in whole-cell models.
extractions.method: Used mitochondrial xenocybrid cells with genetically compromised ETC function, metal-responsive element reporter constructs, and parallel hypoxia experiments to study Cu retention from Cu(atsm).
extractions.finding: Compromised ETC function leads to upregulated glycolysis, NADH accumulation, and increased intracellular Cu retention through dissociation of Cu(atsm), confirmed in both xenocybrid and hypoxic cell models.
extractions.dataset: Mitochondrial xenocybrid cells, control cells, hypoxic cell cultures
extractions.metric: Intracellular Cu retention, bioavailable Cu levels, NADH levels
extractions.baseline: Control cells with normal ETC function
extractions.result: Xenocybrid cells retained more Cu than controls; confirmed bioavailable Cu increase via reporter

extractions.problem: The essential factors for improvement of hyperglycemia by bone marrow transplantation in streptozotocin-induced diabetic mice are not fully elucidated.
extractions.method: Compared whole body, abdomen-only, and abdomen-excluded irradiation with or without bone marrow cell infusion in STZ diabetic mice; tested on beta cell-specific Vegfa knockout mice.
extractions.finding: Whole body irradiation alone (without bone marrow cells) is sufficient to restore beta cell mass; VEGF-A from beta cells is essential for this effect via islet revascularization.
extractions.dataset: C57Bl/6 mice, STZ-induced diabetes, beta cell-specific Vegfa knockout mice
extractions.metric: Blood glucose levels, relative beta cell mass, islet vascularization
extractions.result: Whole body irradiation alone improved hyperglycemia and partially restored islet mass

extractions.problem: Lack of a genetically tractable whole-cell system for studying the phagocyte NADPH oxidase, as native phagocytes are resistant to genetic manipulation.
extractions.method: Functionally reconstituted the NADPH oxidase in COS-7 cells by transfection of gp91phox, p22phox, p47phox, and p67phox; characterized activation, membrane translocation, and phosphorylation mutants.
extractions.finding: COS-phox cells achieved up to 80% of neutrophil superoxide production (arachidonic acid-induced) and recapitulated key regulatory features including p47phox phosphorylation dependence and Rac involvement.
extractions.dataset: COS-7 monkey kidney cells, human neutrophils
extractions.metric: NADPH oxidase activity (superoxide production), % of neutrophil activity
extractions.result: Up to 80% of neutrophil activity (AA-induced), 20% (PMA-induced)

extractions.problem: Understanding durability and lifetime of fuel cell systems requires detailed 3D multiphysics modeling of coupled processes across all system components.
extractions.method: 3D full-scale system-level multiphysics model using coupled CFD-FEM for thermomechanical fatigue analysis under cyclic load conditions.
extractions.finding: The model predicts life expectation and identifies critical regions for thermomechanical fatigue, supporting commercialization of fuel cell technology.
extractions.dataset: Fuel cell system (stack, reformer, afterburner, heat exchanger)
extractions.metric: Thermomechanical fatigue behavior, life expectation
extractions.result: Life expectation predicted using coupled CFD-FEM system model

extractions.problem: Characterizing genetic fidelity of patient-derived xenografts (PDXs) for functional breast cancer studies and identifying endocrine-therapy-resistance mechanisms.
extractions.method: Whole-genome comparison of PDXs with originating breast cancers (structural, copy number, single-nucleotide variants), combined with endocrine-therapy response studies.
extractions.finding: Structural and copy number aberrations were retained with high fidelity in PDXs; ER+ PDXs carried ESR1 mutations, amplification, or translocation producing distinct endocrine-resistance phenotypes not captured by standard cell lines.
extractions.dataset: Breast cancer PDXs across major intrinsic subtypes
extractions.metric: Variant allele frequency preservation, clonal representation
extractions.baseline: Originating tumor genome, standard cell lines
extractions.result: High fidelity retention of structural/copy number aberrations; ESR1 resistance variants identified

extractions.problem: Understanding the biological roles of PARP-1 beyond DNA repair, as revealed by three independent PARP-1 knockout mouse models.
extractions.method: Review of studies using three independently generated PARP-1 deficient mouse models created by homologous recombination, compared with chemical PARP inhibitor approaches.
extractions.finding: PARP-1 knockout confirmed its caretaker function under genotoxic stress and unexpectedly revealed roles in cell death after ischemia-reperfusion and inflammation; residual PARP activity was attributed to PARP-2.
extractions.dataset: Three independent PARP-1 knockout mouse models
extractions.result: Confirmed caretaker function; revealed ischemia-reperfusion and inflammation roles

extractions.problem: How translation and ribosome abundance regulate bacterial cell size and shape under growth perturbations is poorly understood.
extractions.method: Developed a whole-cell model linking ribosomal abundance with cell geometry, division control, and extracellular environment for rod-shaped bacteria, calibrated with experimental E. coli data.
extractions.finding: Cell size maintenance requires a balanced trade-off between ribosome and division protein synthesis; deviations under translation inhibition produce distinct morphological changes matching single-cell experimental data.
extractions.dataset: Escherichia coli single-cell data
extractions.metric: Cell size, growth rate, morphological changes
extractions.result: Model predictions match single-cell E. coli morphological data under translation inhibition

extractions.problem: Determining the role of UNC-13 protein in synaptic vesicle fusion and release at the C. elegans neuromuscular junction.
extractions.method: Whole-cell voltage-clamp electrophysiology of GABAergic and cholinergic synapses in unc-13 mutant C. elegans, combined with electron microscopy and pharmacological assays.
extractions.finding: UNC-13 is essential for synaptic vesicle fusion: unc-13 null mutants have 2-3x more vesicles but almost absent evoked release, and morphologically docked vesicles are not competent for release.
extractions.dataset: C. elegans neuromuscular junction (wild-type and unc-13 mutants)
extractions.metric: Evoked release amplitude, vesicle density, spontaneous release
extractions.result: 2-3x more vesicles but near-zero evoked release in unc-13 nulls

extractions.problem: Developing a structured mathematical model of substrate-limited bacterial growth in continuous culture based on feedback control of RNA and protein synthesis.
extractions.method: Mathematical model with two interconnected feedback control systems (substrate concentration and intracellular building block concentration), simulating biomass, RNA, protein, and substrate dynamics.
extractions.finding: Three types of steady-states exist in continuous culture; the model describes dynamic responses to step changes in dilution rate, substrate concentration, and temperature, with RNA/protein ratio as a physiological state measure.
extractions.dataset: Continuous bacterial culture (theoretical model)
extractions.metric: RNA/protein ratio, growth rate, steady-state stability
extractions.result: Three steady-state types identified; model describes responses to environmental perturbations

extractions.problem: Characterizing the properties of sodium and calcium channels in neuroendocrine chromaffin cells.
extractions.method: Patch-clamp technique (cell-attached, outside-out, and whole-cell configurations) with pharmacological isolation of Na+ and Ca2+ currents using Co2+ and TTX.
extractions.finding: Na+ channels have ~1 pA elementary current at -10 mV with density 1.5-10/um2; Ca2+ channels have density 5-15/um2, show minimal inactivation, and require a two-Lorentzian kinetic model; Ca2+ channel properties depend on permeant ion identity.
extractions.dataset: Bovine adrenal chromaffin cells
extractions.metric: Single-channel amplitude, open time, channel density, kinetic time constants
extractions.result: Na+ channel: 1 pA at -10 mV, 1 ms open time; Ca2+ channel: 0.9 pA at -5 mV, density 5-15/um2
